message:
fix(websocket): accept RFC-reserved close codes on receive, enforce 125-byte close payload limit

frame.rs Bug 1 (MEDIUM): validate_close_payload used is_valid_code()
which rejects close codes 1016-2999 from peers. RFC 6455 §7.4.2
reserves this range for future revisions and extensions — a conforming
endpoint must accept them even if it does not recognise the value.
Added is_valid_received_code() that accepts 1000-1003 + 1007-4999
(excluding wire-forbidden 1004-1006, 1015). Send-side validation
unchanged.

frame.rs Bug 2 (MEDIUM): Frame::close() did not enforce the 125-byte
control frame payload limit (RFC 6455 §5.5). A reason string >123
bytes would create a structurally invalid Frame that only fails at
encode time. Added assertion at construction.

Also audited SOUND: reactor/registration.rs (838L).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
